title: pittosporum terminalioides
intro: Pittosporum terminalioides, also known as the New Zealand tobacco plant, is a flowering plant native to the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. Its health benefits include aiding in the relief of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as exhibiting antiseptic and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum terminalioides are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use.
